workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Can you extract the deployment blockers from this support ticket for Fabrikam Residences? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 13:36 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fabrikam Residences deployment blockers

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 10:15 EST
Subject: Re: Re: deployment blockers Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fabrikam Residences: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-P trap (Separation of follow-up draft from pipeline note forces context-switching); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-P failure mode (Separation of follow-up draft from pipeline note forces context-switching)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-P -- Separation of follow-up draft from pipeline note forces context-switching
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
